observations: All entries share the same project value 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'
The listed paths span HTML, JavaScript, TypeScript declaration, JSX, Markdown, and CSS files
It includes both a type declaration (Slide.d.ts) and a component implementation (Slide.jsx) for the same conceptual entity
It includes a DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md documentation file and a preview/_card.css stylesheet
The file contains no symbols array content
standouts: Co-listing of Slide.d.ts and Slide.jsx suggests a type-plus-implementation pair
Inclusion of DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md alongside code files ties documentation to implementation
The batch covers core slide-related files plus a preview stylesheet
notes: A small, root-mapped manifest of claude-ds files selected for batch interpretation, with no extracted code symbols.
questions: What criteria determined that these six files belong together in batch-027?
Does the order of entries carry significance?

purpose_vs_actual: The path frames it as an interpretive allocation batch, and the content is exactly a list of files allocated for interpretation, so purpose matches actual content.
intention_signals: It appears to define a scoped work package of related claude-ds files so an interpretive or fusion process can analyze them as a unit.
contribution: It contributes a project-rooted, file-level manifest that a fused system can use as an input set for an interpretive batch, preserving the original grouping and source locations.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds project files across HTML, JS, TS declaration, JSX, Markdown, and CSS with their common root directory and no extracted symbols.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to select and register a small coherent set of claude-ds source files for an interpretive ledger pass.
